title: Air-fryer fish fingers
subtitle: Homemade panko-crumbed fish fingers without deep-frying
excerpt: Cod fillet cut into strips, coated in seasoned flour, egg, and panko breadcrumbs, and cooked in the air fryer at 200°C for 10 to 12 minutes. The panko crust is crispier than the coating on a frozen fish finger and the fish inside is fresh rather than reformed.
sourceNotes: The fish finger was invented by Birds Eye in the UK in 1955. The original product uses reformed fish in a breadcrumb coating designed for deep-frying or oven cooking. Homemade fish fingers use a single fillet cut into strips and coated in the same way as schnitzel or chicken tenders. The air fryer replicates deep-frying without the oil.

content.text: Spray the coated fish fingers generously with oil before cooking. Panko breadcrumbs contain no fat and will not brown without it. A generous oil spray on all sides before the air fryer produces a golden, crisp coating. A light mist is not enough: the breadcrumb surface needs to be fully coated.

items.name: Smoked paprika
items.prepNote: for colour, mixed into the breadcrumbs

content.text: Pat the {{cod-fillet}} strips dry with kitchen paper. Set up three shallow bowls: seasoned {{plain-flour}}, beaten {{eggs}}, and {{breadcrumbs-panko}} mixed with {{smoked-paprika}} and {{sea-salt-fine}}. Coat each strip in flour first, shaking off the excess, then egg, then press firmly into the breadcrumbs. Preheat the air fryer to 200°C for 2 minutes. Spray the basket lightly with oil. Place the fish fingers in a single layer, not touching. Spray the tops generously with oil. Cook at 200°C for 10 to 12 minutes, turning carefully at the 6-minute mark and spraying the other side with oil. They are done when deep golden and the fish is opaque and flaking throughout.

items.fix: The panko must be coated with oil to brown. Spray generously before cooking and again after turning. Cook in a single layer with gaps between each finger; overcrowding produces steam instead of crunch
items.cause: Insufficient oil spray, or basket overcrowded
items.symptom: Breadcrumb coating pale and soft after 12 minutes

items.fix: A light flour coat is enough; shake off the excess. Make sure the egg wash touches every part of the floured surface before pressing into the breadcrumbs. Press the panko firmly against the fish
items.cause: Flour layer too thick, or egg wash missed spots
items.symptom: Coating falling off in the basket

items.fix: Cut strips to about 2 cm wide and 1 cm thick. Very thin pieces cook in 8 minutes. Check at 10 minutes by breaking one open: the fish should be just opaque all the way through, not dry and flaking apart
items.cause: Fish strips too thin, or cooked too long
items.symptom: Fish overcooked and dry inside a crisp coating

content.text: Air-fryer fish fingers are a weekend lunch and a quick children's dinner. Serve with chips and peas in the classic British arrangement, or in a wrap with tartare sauce and lettuce. The homemade version has a far better texture than the frozen equivalent and takes less time to prepare and cook than the oven-baked version from frozen.
